mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0 percent, it signals severe labor market slack and collapsing consumer spending, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (out-of-sample hit rate < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during expansionary macro regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion periods (Rank IC < 0.01) or predicts negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across all historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 220.0, it signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging trends where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirm genuine labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate hiring, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, forcing equity markets to downgrade earnings forecasts and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns (hit rate < 45%).
